name: First Mutual Bank, Fsb
description_short: First Mutual Bank is a mutual savings bank offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and home equity lines of credit with a community-focused ownership model.
description_long:
First Mutual Bank, FSB operates as a mutual savings bank, meaning it is owned by its depositors rather than external shareholders. This ownership structure shapes the bank's stated mission to prioritize community development and member service over profit maximization. The bank maintains an active online presence at firstmutual.bank and provides digital banking infrastructure including mobile apps and online account management.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including checking accounts with multiple tiers, savings accounts, and certificate of deposit (CD) products. Their lending services include mortgages (advertised at 5.99% fixed for 30 years during promotional periods), home equity lines of credit (HELOC) with introductory rates, and home equity products. They also provide mobile banking capabilities through apps on both iOS and Android platforms, along with standard online banking enrollment and password recovery services.

First Mutual Bank differentiates itself through its mutual ownership structure, which it actively promotes as "the Mutual Bank Difference." The bank emphasizes that as a depositor-owned institution, customer deposits are reinvested directly into served communities rather than distributed to external shareholders. This positioning appeals to customers seeking community-focused banking relationships. The bank also advertises competitive promotional rates on mortgages and CDs to attract deposits and new borrowers.

As a regional mutual savings bank, First Mutual Bank serves a traditional banking function but operates within a narrower geographic footprint than national banks. Customers should verify branch and service availability in their area, as the website references "Find a Location" functionality suggesting limited branch networks. The promotional rates advertised are time-limited offers, and actual approved rates will vary based on creditworthiness and market conditions. Prospective customers should review specific terms, fees, and account requirements before opening accounts. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Online banking account enrollment and management
Online password recovery and login assistance
Digital statements and tax form access (eStatements and paper options)
Account routing number and bank identification
pros: Mutual ownership structure reinvests deposits back into local communities rather than shareholder profits
Competitive promotional mortgage rates (5.99% 30-year fixed advertised) and HELOC introductory offers (4.99% APR)
Mobile banking apps available on both iOS and Android for account access and management
Multiple checking account tiers to accommodate different banking needs and preferences
CD products with competitive APY rates (3.50% and 3.25% advertised for 7 and 13-month terms)
Online account enrollment and digital banking options reduce need for branch visits
FDIC-regulated savings bank with routing number and NMLS licensing (1805397) indicating regulatory compliance
cons: Promotional rates are time-limited and actual approved rates depend on individual creditworthiness and market conditions
Website indicates limited branch locations ("Find a Location" suggests regional rather than national coverage)
No mention of account fee structures, minimum balance requirements, or overdraft policies on website
Limited product diversity compared to full-service banks; no credit card, personal loan, or business lending products mentioned
Customer service contact information and phone number not prominently displayed on homepage
best_for: Community-minded depositors seeking mutual bank ownership and local reinvestment of deposits
Homebuyers and homeowners interested in mortgages and home equity products during promotional periods
Savers looking for competitive CD rates and interest-bearing checking/savings accounts
Customers in the bank's service area seeking traditional full-service banking with digital convenience
diagnosis: First Mutual Bank is best for depositors seeking traditional banking services with a community-focused, mutual ownership model and those in its service area interested in home lending products during promotional periods. Primary caveats include limited geographic availability (regional rather than national coverage), time-limited promotional rates that vary by creditworthiness, and lack of information regarding fees and account requirements on the public website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
